blurb: Chubby, velvety leaves tipped with a row of reddish 'claws' — unmistakably little bear paws. Fuzzy all over and irresistibly pettable; branches into a small bushy plant.

care.light: Bright light with a little gentle sun keeps it compact and reddens the claw tips; low light makes it leggy and drops leaves.
care.water: Soak-and-dry, and cautious — the fuzzy leaves hate sitting wet and drop at the slightest overwatering. Water the soil, not the leaves.
care.temperature: Room temperature; keep above ~40°F and dry when cool.
care.dormancy: Tends to a cool-season grower and slows in the heat of summer — ease off water in the hottest spell.
care.feeding: A dilute feed once or twice during active growth.
care.potting: Repot every couple of years; prune leggy stems to keep the bushy shape.
care.propagation: Stem cuttings root well; leaves are less reliable — let cuttings callus first.
care.bloom: Clusters of orange, bell-shaped flowers on tall stalks on mature plants.
care.troubles: Leaf drop from overwatering or low light; mealybugs tucked in the fuzz.
care.toxicity: Toxic — Cotyledon contain cardiac glycosides (bufadienolides); poisonous to animals if eaten. Keep it away from cats, dogs, and children.

sources.title: Cotyledon tomentosa subsp. tomentosa (Bear's Paw) — RHS
Cotyledon tomentosa — LLIFLE Encyclopedia of Succulents
